name: Marty Napoleon
abstract: Marty Napoleon (June 2, 1921 – April 27, 2015) was an American jazz pianist. He replaced Earl Hines in Louis Armstrong's All Stars band in 1952.

interviews.title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dody Goodman in "Dody in Dixieland"

works.t: Baby, Won't You Please Come Home

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing there with a trio, Marty Napoleon’s trio. Marty, myself and a guy by the name of Gary Chester on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: And after Joe Sullivan came in they wanted me. So this is pre-Napoleon. >> [INTERVIEWER] Marty Napoleon. [INTERVIEWEE] Martin Napoleon. This was pre-Marty --

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: Gene Krupa had his trio then, and it featured Charlie Ventura on tenor saxophone and a pianist, Marty Napoleon.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: There's Dill Jones, there's Marty Napoleon. ... I look and there's one of the guys that I know

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Longo
about_quotes.evidence: Marty Napoleon was Louis Armstrong’s pianist at the time. And he was the house pianist there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Longo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Davern
about_quotes.evidence: I went there and I passed the audition and he was going to buy me a bass clarinet
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Davern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]
